ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the tweet was created */,
  "ota" INTEGER /* OTA reference (e.g., brand associated with the tweet) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the tweet */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies */,
  "marketing_campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the marketing campaign associated with the tweet */,
  "source_platform" TEXT /* Social media platform where the tweet was posted */,
  "tweet_content" TEXT /* Full content of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_vader" REAL /* Sentiment score calculated by VADER */,
  "sentiment_textblob" REAL /* Sentiment score calculated by TextBlob */,
  "subjectivity" REAL /* Subjectivity score of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_label" INTEGER /* Labeling of sentiment (1 for positive, 0 for negative) */,
  "cleaned_tweet" TEXT /* Cleaned version of the tweet for analysis */,
  "lemmatized_tweet" TEXT /* Lemmatized version of the tweet for analysis */,
  "tweet_string" TEXT /* String representation of the tweet for export or display */,
  "sentiment_overall" TEXT /* Overall sentiment label (positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "sentiment_confidence" REAL /* Confidence score of the overall sentiment */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Key themes or topics identified in the tweet */,
  "emojis" TEXT /* Emojis used in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_ota FOREIGN KEY ("ota") REFERENCES brands ("brand_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment score entry */,
  "sentiment_algorithm" TEXT /* Name of the sentiment analysis algorithm used (e.g., VADER, TextBlob) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the algorithm and its application */,
  "algorithm_version" TEXT /* Version of the sentiment analysis algorithm */,
  "performance_metrics" TEXT /* Performance metrics of the algorithm (e.g., accuracy, precision, recall) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "brands" (
  "brand_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each brand */,
  "brand_name" TEXT /* Name of the brand */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description and details about the brand */,
  "brand_url" TEXT /* URL of the brand's official website */,
  "brand_social_handles" TEXT /* Social media handles of the brand */,
  "brand_risk_level" INTEGER /* Risk level associated with the brand (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("brand_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, marketing manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., marketing, data analysis) */,
  "access_level" INTEGER /* Access level of the user (e.g., admin, standard) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being analyzed or acted upon */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken (e.g., 'analyzed', 'flagged') */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action was performed */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments by the user */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., analyze, flag, report) */,
  "sentiment_change" TEXT /* Change in sentiment (e.g., improved, declined, stable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_logs_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
